chapter_context.summary: While his army is at war, David commits adultery with Bathsheba; he then conspires to have her husband Uriah the Hittite killed in battle.

outline.text: David Stays in Jerusalem; Adultery with Bathsheba
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David Stays in Jerusalem; Adultery with Bathsheba (11:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: David Summons Uriah to Cover the Sin; Uriah's Loyalty
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David Summons Uriah to Cover the Sin; Uriah's Loyalty (11:6-13), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: David's Letter to Joab; Uriah Abandoned and Slain in Battle
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David's Letter to Joab; Uriah Abandoned and Slain in Battle (11:14-25), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: Bathsheba Mourns Uriah; David Marries Bathsheba; God's Displeasure
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Bathsheba Mourns Uriah; David Marries Bathsheba; God's Displeasure (11:26-27), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

key_themes.title: The Davidic Covenant
key_themes.description: God's unconditional promise of an eternal dynasty, ultimately pointing forward to the Messiah.

key_themes.title: Sin and Consequences
key_themes.description: While God offers complete forgiveness for sincere repentance, the earthly consequences of sin remain.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 11 demonstrate the grace of God in the midst of human failure?
What lessons about leadership and accountability can you apply to your own life from this chapter?

cultural_historical.title: Jerusalem as Capital
cultural_historical.text: By capturing Jerusalem, a neutral city on the border of Judah and Benjamin, David strategically united the fractured northern and southern tribes under a single capital.
